name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Sacramento, Ca
description_short: NACA is a nonprofit housing advocacy organization providing HUD-approved housing counseling and character-based mortgage lending to underserved communities, having served 3 million people.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years working as a housing advocacy nonprofit. The organization fights predatory lending practices while providing affordable homeownership solutions to historically excluded communities. NACA operates on a mission to close the racial wealth disparity gap through character-based lending that considers borrowers' circumstances rather than credit scores.

NACA offers a signature mortgage product called the Best in America Mortgage, featuring no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and below-market fixed rates (currently 5.625% for 30-year, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year as of April 6, 2026). They provide HUD-approved housing counseling—accounting for 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally—and have helped 500,000 homeowners achieve ownership while facilitating $20 billion in mortgage commitments. The organization also operates Achieve the Dream Events in multiple cities where prospective members can access services and become NACA Qualified. Additionally, NACA advocates against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors.

What distinguishes NACA is their character-based lending approach that explicitly ignores credit scores and their deep roots as a member-driven advocacy organization. Rather than functioning as a traditional lender, NACA combines counseling, advocacy, and lending through partnerships with institutional lenders. They operate a volunteer network (NACtivist program) and maintain a strong focus on racial equity and economic justice. Their 75,000 mortgages originated represent direct impact on wealth-building in underserved communities.

The main caveat is that NACA is a nonprofit advocacy organization, not a traditional mortgage lender—they facilitate mortgages through partnerships and require members to participate in their program and attend events. Their services are concentrated in specific geographic areas where they hold Achieve the Dream Events, limiting accessibility for those unable to attend multi-day events. While they emphasize affordability, qualification still requires meeting their membership criteria, and the organization's advocacy-focused mission means they prioritize systemic change alongside individual lending.
pros: No down payment, no closing costs, no mortgage insurance, and no credit score consideration on mortgages
Below-market fixed rates (5.625% for 30-year mortgages as of April 2026)
HUD-approved housing counseling—provides 30% of all national HUD housing counseling
Character-based lending that considers borrowers' full circumstances rather than credit history
Nonprofit mission focused on closing racial wealth disparity gap with 36-year track record
Free member portal to upload documents and check application status
Established credibility: 500,000 homeowners helped, $20 billion in mortgage commitments, 75,000 NACA mortgages originated
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day Achieve the Dream Events (Friday-Sunday) to access services and become qualified
Services are geographically limited to event locations (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis, etc.)
As a nonprofit facilitator rather than direct lender, the process may involve longer timelines coordinating with partner lenders
Member-based model requires active participation and commitment beyond typical loan application
Limited detail on website about specific eligibility requirements beyond character-based assessment
best_for: First-time homebuyers with poor or no credit history who can attend in-person events
Borrowers in underserved communities seeking below-market mortgage rates without down payment
Individuals interested in homeownership combined with housing advocacy and community involvement
Minorities and historically excluded groups targeted by predatory lending
diagnosis: NACA is best for first-time homebuyers with limited credit history who can commit to attending a multi-day in-person event and want to combine homeownership with community advocacy. The primary caveat is geographic and time constraints—you must be able to travel to and attend a weekend Achieve the Dream Event in one of their designated cities to access services.
services: Character-based mortgage lending ignoring credit scores
Achieve the Dream Event weekend programs for prospective members
Member portal for document upload and status tracking
NACtivist volunteer program and community organizing
Partner lender coordination for mortgage fulfillment
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
